faq_1_q: So... what is Career CoPilot, actually?
faq_1_a: Career CoPilot brings resume analysis, interview practice, career-path drafts, application writing, and job-search tools into one workspace. AI scores and suggestions are decision-support drafts: review them against the original resume, job description, and current market information.
faq_2_q: How does the resume analysis actually work?
faq_2_a: Upload a supported PDF, DOCX, image, or text resume. After processing, Career CoPilot produces an AI-generated score, strengths, issues, keywords, and suggested edits based on the selected market. This is not a recruiter decision or an outcome guarantee; verify every suggestion against the source resume and target role.
faq_3_q: Can I really practice interviews here?
faq_3_a: Paste a job description to generate role-specific practice questions. You can answer by text or, where the browser supports it, by voice, then review STAR-oriented feedback after the session. The feedback is practice guidance and does not predict how a real interviewer will assess you.
faq_4_q: I'm thinking about switching careers. Can this help?
faq_4_a: Career Path Planner compares the information in your resume with a target role and drafts possible gaps, bridge roles, and phased next steps. Requirements and timelines can be incomplete or outdated, so confirm them against current job postings and professional sources before acting.
faq_5_q: What sources does the Opportunity Finder use?
faq_5_a: Opportunity Finder combines active Career CoPilot employer postings with AI-generated suggestions. External results depend on the configured search grounding and may be incomplete or stale. Before applying, verify the role, requirements, closing date, and link on the employer's original site. Match analysis is advisory.
faq_6_q: I'm applying to jobs in another country. Can you help?
faq_6_a: Select a target market to generate a resume draft using the configured conventions for that market. Local practices vary by employer and change over time; review the draft with current local guidance. The output does not guarantee ATS parsing, interviews, or recruiter acceptance.
faq_7_q: Is my data safe with you?
faq_7_a: When you run a tool, submitted content may be processed by the configured AI and infrastructure providers. Employer workflows share application or profile information according to your actions and consent. We use access controls and encryption in transit, but no internet service can guarantee absolute security. Read the Privacy Policy for providers, retention, and deletion details.
faq_8_q: What does the free plan include?
faq_8_a: You get 150 starter credits, enough to try several core workflows such as resume analysis, a cover letter, and interview practice. Free accounts also receive a small monthly refill for light use, with a daily run cap to keep the service sustainable.
faq_9_q: How does the Portfolio Website Builder work?
faq_9_a: Upload your resume, review the generated showcase, and download the HTML file. You choose and configure any external hosting; Career CoPilot does not automatically create a public URL. Check every generated fact and remove sensitive information before publishing.
faq_10_q: How do Verified Talent credentials work?
faq_10_a: When Web3 identity is enabled and the configured eligibility rule is met, you can issue an optional Proof-of-Talent credential signal and choose whether to show it. It is not independent verification of identity, employment, or skill, and it does not imply a hiring outcome.
faq_11_q: What about the English Pro Coach and Certification Prep?
faq_11_a: English Pro provides AI feedback on professional writing, reading, listening, and speaking practice. Certification Prep generates unofficial Agile practice questions and explanations. Neither tool is an official language assessment or certification exam; verify current requirements and syllabi with the relevant authority.
faq_12_q: Can this help with networking and professional communication?
faq_12_a: The Email Drafter prepares reviewable thank-you, follow-up, and networking drafts. The Networking Assistant suggests contact types and outreach approaches; it does not identify or verify specific people or their contact details. Check every fact and recipient before sending.
faq_13_q: Can I use this for my current job, not just job hunting?
faq_13_a: Performance Review Prep and Learning Roadmap generate drafts from the information you provide. Event Scout suggests events, but dates, links, eligibility, and availability can change. Verify event details with the organizer before making plans or paying.

resume_market_hint_canada: Canadian/North American style: 1–2 page reverse-chronological resume, no photo or protected personal details, strong action verbs with source-supported metrics.
resume_market_hint_united_states: Common US style: 1–2 page reverse-chronological resume, no photo or protected personal details, a dedicated Skills section, and a simple single-column structure. Employer and ATS requirements vary.
resume_market_hint_united_kingdom: UK CV: usually up to 2 pages, opens with a concise Personal Statement/Profile, no photo by default, British English spelling throughout.
resume_market_hint_germany: German Lebenslauf-inspired CV: structured A4 layout and formal tone. Preserve personal details only if already supplied; do not invent photo, birth, nationality, or marital fields.
resume_market_hint_france: French CV: concise A4 CV with formal register. Photo/personal details vary by context; preserve only source-supported details and keep recruiter readability first.
resume_market_hint_japan: Japan: produce a conservative 職務経歴書-style career history. A true 履歴書 is a separate standardized form; do not fake photo or personal-data fields.
resume_market_hint_china: China resume (简历): 1–2 page reverse-chronological 简历 in Simplified Chinese; employer and school names localized with the original in parentheses. Include only source-supported personal details — no fabricated photo, age, or marital status.
resume_market_hint_vietnam: Vietnam resume: 1–2 pages, reverse-chronological, with certifications, technical skills, and English proficiency only when supported by the source resume.
resume_market_hint_singapore: Singapore style: Western-format resume, no photo by default, ATS-friendly, with work-rights/visa status only if the resume already provides it.
resume_market_hint_australia: Australian resume: achievement-led Western format, no photo by default, 2–3 pages acceptable for experienced profiles, work-rights only when source-supported.
resume_market_principle_north_america_1: Simple single-column layout; verify target ATS requirements
resume_market_principle_north_america_2: No photo or protected personal details
resume_market_principle_north_america_3: Action-led bullets with metrics where supported
resume_market_principle_europe_3: Privacy-aware: no invented photo or personal-data fields
resume_market_principle_apac_3: Work-rights line when relevant and source-supported
resume_market_principle_japan_3: Does not fake 履歴書 photo/personal fields

mi_faq_q1: How is this different from a general chat tool?
mi_faq_a1: It uses your resume and target role to generate timed practice questions, scores answers with a consistent AI rubric, and returns strengths, gaps, and suggested revisions. The report is practice guidance, not an employer assessment.
mi_faq_q2: What problems does it actually solve?
mi_faq_a2: It gives you role-specific questions, a consistent structure for reviewing your answers, and timed rehearsal. It cannot know which questions a real interviewer will ask or guarantee an interview outcome.
mi_faq_q3: How are the practice questions chosen?
mi_faq_a3: The AI generates questions from the resume and role context you provide. A named project or skill gap may become a follow-up, but the questions are not sourced from an employer or a prediction of its interview.
mi_error_context_required: Provide at least one of: job description, responsibilities, or requirements.
mi_flow_note: Timed practice pacing: 15 seconds to prepare, then 3 minutes to answer. Feedback appears after the session; this format does not reproduce any employer's interview process.
mi_disclaimer_p1: The interviewer is a virtual practice persona, not a real person; any resemblance to actual individuals is coincidental.
mi_disclaimer_p2: Questions and evaluations are generated for practice only. They are not professional advice, do not represent any real company's hiring standards, and imply no employment outcome.
mi_disclaimer_p3: Your answers, including voice transcripts, are sent to the configured AI provider for evaluation and may be saved in your interview history. See the Privacy Policy for processing, retention, and deletion details.
mi_disclaimer_p4: The timed format can feel stressful — you may end the interview at any time; answered questions will still be evaluated.

Hi {candidate},

I am reaching out from {company} about {role}. Your profile looks relevant to the role, and I would like to request access to your contact packet so we can discuss fit and next steps.

If you are open to it, please accept this request in Career CoPilot. Your contact details and resume will only be shared after you approve.

site_sample_analysis_summary: Alex's resume is technically readable and shows strong delivery experience, but the current positioning still reads more like a software profile than a product management submission.
site_sample_strength_1: Clear single-column structure designed to reduce common ATS parsing risks; verify it in the target system.
site_sample_strength_2: Strong technical delivery evidence across Agile, SQL, Jira, and cross-functional work.
site_sample_strength_3: One measurable business outcome is already present and can anchor the rewrite.
site_sample_improve_1_tip: Lead with a bridge title such as Technical Product Owner instead of only Software Developer.
site_sample_improve_2_tip: Rewrite the top three bullets with one metric each: customer count, ticket reduction, delivery time, or revenue scope.
site_sample_improve_3_tip: Add evidence of customer interviews, problem validation, prioritization, and roadmap tradeoffs.
site_wf_js_p1_r1_v: Title does not match target role
site_wf_js_p2_r1_v: Billing workflow redesign maps to product ops
site_wf_js_p3_r2_v: Result line needs one measurable outcome
site_wf_js_p4_r3_v: Rewrite bullets and apply to 5 bridge roles
site_wf_emp_p1_r1_v: Role, must-haves, and screeners captured as data
site_wf_emp_p1_r2_v: Two biased phrases flagged and rewritten
site_wf_emp_p1_r3_v: Live on the candidate job feed
site_wf_emp_p2_r1_v: Led checkout redesign — maps to your funnel role
site_wf_emp_p4_r1_v: Contact unlocked only after candidate accepts
site_sample_generation_flow_desc: This mirrors the signed-in analysis flow: read the resume, check its structure, compare it with the target role, and assemble the report.
site_sample_english_market_note: The resume excerpt and ATS keywords are intentionally shown in English because this sample targets an English-language Product Manager role.
site_sample_fit_headline: Product Manager fit is close, but the positioning needs work.
site_sample_cta_title: Run this on your own resume
site_sample_cta_desc: Upload a resume to get a structured score, suggested fixes, keywords, and next-step tools in your workspace. Review generated output before using it.
site_sample_rewrite_example: Led discovery for a billing workflow redesign; interviewed 12 customers, prioritized the backlog with engineering and design, and reduced support tickets by 18% in Q2.
site_sample_issue_1_why: Product recruiters look for evidence that you validated the problem before building; feature lists alone read like an engineering handoff.
site_sample_issue_1_fix: Add one bullet with the customer interviews, insight gathered, and decision made.
site_sample_issue_2_why: Product screens expect several metrics across impact, scope, and collaboration; sparse numbers weaken the seniority signal.
site_sample_issue_2_fix: Convert the top three bullets to STAR format with one metric each, such as percentage, revenue, time saved, or users affected.
site_sample_issue_3_why: Role keyword filters may exclude this resume before a person reads it because the current title emphasizes software development.
site_sample_issue_3_fix: Use “Software Developer · Product-focused” or lead with a bridge title such as “Technical Product Owner.”
site_interview_sample_question: Tell me about a time you had to prioritize conflicting requests from stakeholders.
site_interview_sample_answer: The candidate described a sprint where sales and support wanted different fixes, and prioritized support because ticket volume was rising.
site_interview_sample_situation: Clear — sprint pressure and two competing requests.
site_interview_sample_task: Partial — ownership of the prioritization is implied but not stated.
site_interview_sample_action: Good start — the decision criterion, ticket volume, is explicit.
site_interview_sample_result: Weak — there is no metric for ticket reduction or business impact.
site_interview_sample_missing: Add how the tradeoff was communicated to sales and what was deferred.
site_interview_sample_next: Retry with one number in the Result line and a one-sentence example of stakeholder communication.
